flags.issue: The deposit of S$9,000 equals two months' rent, above the one-month norm; the 24-month term is also longer than the typical 12-month tenancy.
flags.suggestion: Confirm with the landlord that a two-month deposit and a 24-month fixed term are intended before sending.

flags.issue: The diplomatic clause allows early termination only after twelve (12) months, but the fixed term is 24 months, leaving a long non-terminable initial period relative to a standard tenancy.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the twelve-month minimum stay and two-month notice suit both parties given the two-year term.

flags.issue: The deposit of S$10,400 equals two months' rent, above the one-month norm for a 12-month term.
flags.suggestion: Confirm with the landlord that a two-month deposit is intended before sending.

flags.issue: The diplomatic clause allows early termination only after twelve (12) months, but the Term is exactly 12 months, leaving effectively no window to invoke it.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the minimum-stay period with the landlord; a 12-month minimum on a 12-month lease renders the diplomatic clause largely inoperative.

flags.issue: The deposit of S$17,000 equals two months' rent. For a 36-month term a two-month deposit is common, but confirm this matches what was agreed.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the two-month deposit amount with the landlord before sending.

flags.issue: The fixed term is 36 months (three years), which is longer than the typical one- to two-year residential tenancy and there is no diplomatic or break clause included.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the parties intend a three-year fixed term and consider whether an early-termination provision should be added.

flags.issue: This is a room-only rental (master bedroom), but the clause bodies assume the whole HDB flat is let to the Tenant.
flags.suggestion: Add a special condition stating exactly which room is let (master bedroom) and which areas (e.g. kitchen, bathroom, living room) are shared, and note any occupancy limits for the flat as a whole.

flags.issue: The Inventory and handover clause assumes possession of the entire Premises, which does not fit a room rental where the landlord or others also occupy the flat.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the Inventory covers only the let room and shared items the Tenant may use, and clarify access arrangements to shared areas.

flags.issue: For a room rental, requiring the Tenant to register utility accounts in their own name and pay all utilities is usually impractical since utilities are shared with other occupants.
flags.suggestion: Confirm with the landlord how utilities are apportioned (e.g. included in rent or split by share) and add a special condition to reflect this.

flags.issue: Renting out a room in an HDB flat is subject to HDB subletting-of-room rules, including eligibility and maximum occupancy limits.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the landlord has met HDB's conditions for renting out a room (owner's minimum occupation period, registration of tenants, occupancy cap) before signing.
